Notice Of Motion To Compel Third Party Discovery From Fidelity National And Coldwell Bankers
Off Calendar Feb-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CHARLES FERGUSON'S Motion To Compel Third Party Discovery From Fidelity National And Coldwell Bankers is denied. Mr. Ferguson's briefing focuses on balancing the relevance of the information sought against the privacy interests of Ms. Gatti, an issue Mr. Ferguson contends already has been decided by the Court in his favor. In response to Fidelity's argument that Mr. Ferguson did not provide a Notice to Consumer under CCP section 1985.3 , Mr. Ferguson responds that Ms. Gatti is a party to this action and in any event was served with the subpoena and a Notice to Consumer. Even assuming that Mr. Ferguson is correct as to all of that, there is no record that Notice to Consumer was given to the sellers, who are not parties to this action. The evidence submitted by Fidelity establishes that Mr. Ferguson's subpoena requests the sellers' personal records and information. Mr. Ferguson does not address the issue of the sellers' privacy or provide evidence that Notice to Consumer was given to the sellers. Mr. Ferguson's motion to compel is denied, without prejudice of course to him re-serving the subpoena with proper notice to or authorization from the sellers. The prevailing party is to provide a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling by way of e mail to the Judge Pro Tem. Opposing parties must be included in the e mail.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Huibonhoa. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (302)
